top_signals.title: Physical Crude Cargoes Drop as Middle Eastern Supply Rises
top_signals.summary: Physical crude oil cargoes are selling at steep discounts around the world, changing trade flows as markets come under pressure from fast-rising Middle Eastern supply with Iran set to boost sales following a temporary reprieve from U.S. sanctions.The discounts…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US Senate votes to halt Iran war in latest rebuke of Trump - Reuters
top_signals.summary: US Senate votes to halt Iran war in latest rebuke of Trump  Reuters
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Trump Says Justice Department Looking Into Gasoline Prices
top_signals.summary: US President Donald Trump said he has ordered the Department of Justice to look into gasoline prices, complaining that they aren’t falling fast enough.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
top_signals.summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin miners face deepening margin squeeze as revenue falls below production costs
top_signals.summary: An estimated 20% of miners are now unprofitable at current prices, and the stress shows up at the network level, too.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
top_signals.summary: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Germany Scraps Frigate Program, Rheinmetall Shares Plummet
top_signals.summary: Germany scrapped a landmark frigate programme on Wednesday following delays and expected cost overruns, sending shares in the country's top defence firm Rheinmetall — which was tipped to win the contract — sharply lower.Plans to build six F126 frigates had been mired in trouble for a while…
